content:
# Judges Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), c. 1008 CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/JDG_page_269.jpg, JDG_page_270.jpg

1. And these [are] the nations which YHWH left to test Yisrael by them — all who had not known all the wars of Canaan —

2. only so that the generations of the sons of Yisrael might know, to teach them war — only those who had not known [them] before:

3. five lords of the Pelishtim, and all the Canaanites, and the Tsidonians, and the Chivvites dwelling in Mount Lebanon, from Mount Baal-Chermon as far as Levo-Chamath.

4. And they were for testing Yisrael, to know whether they would listen to the commandments of YHWH which he had commanded their fathers by the hand of Moshe.

5. And the sons of Yisrael dwelt in the midst of the Canaanite, the Chittite, and the Amorite, and the Perizzite, and the Chivvite, and the Yevusite.

6. And they took their daughters for themselves as wives, and their [own] daughters they gave to their sons, and they served their gods.

7. And the sons of Yisrael did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, and they forgot YHWH their God, and they served the Baalim and the Asheroth.

8. And the anger of YHWH burned against Yisrael, and he sold them into the hand of Cushan-Rishatayim, king of Aram-Naharayim. And the sons of Yisrael served Cushan-Rishatayim eight years.

9. And the sons of Yisrael cried out to YHWH, and YHWH raised up a savior for the sons of Yisrael, and he saved them — Otniel son of Kenaz, the younger brother of Calev.

10. And the Spirit of YHWH was upon him, and he judged Yisrael. And he went out to war, and YHWH gave Cushan-Rishatayim, king of Aram, into his hand, and his hand was strong against Cushan-Rishatayim.

11. And the land had rest forty years. And Otniel son of Kenaz died.

12. And the sons of Yisrael again did the evil in the eyes of YHWH. And YHWH strengthened Eglon king of Moav against Yisrael, because they had done the evil in the eyes of YHWH.

13. And he gathered to himself the sons of Ammon and Amaleq, and he went and struck Yisrael, and they possessed the city of the palms.

14. And the sons of Yisrael served Eglon king of Moav eighteen years.

15. And the sons of Yisrael cried out to YHWH, and YHWH raised up for them a savior — Ehud son of Gera, a Binyaminite, a man restricted in his right hand. And the sons of Yisrael sent by his hand tribute to Eglon king of Moav.

16. And Ehud made for himself a sword — two edges to it, a gomed its length — and he girded it under his garments upon his right thigh.

17. And he brought near the tribute to Eglon king of Moav. And Eglon [was] a very fat man.

18. And it was when he had finished presenting the tribute, he sent away the people who carried the tribute.

19. And he himself turned back from the carved-stones which [were] at Gilgal, and he said, "A secret word I have for you, O king." And he said, "Silence!" And all those standing by him went out from him.

20. And Ehud came to him, and he was sitting in the cool upper-room which [was] for him alone. And Ehud said, "A word of God I have for you." And he arose from the seat.

21. And Ehud stretched out his left hand and took the sword from upon his right thigh and thrust it into his belly.

22. And the handle also went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade, for he did not draw the sword from his belly; and it came out the back.

23. And Ehud went out to the porch, and he closed the doors of the upper-room upon him and locked [them].

24. And he had gone out, and his servants came. And they saw, and behold, the doors of the upper-room [were] locked. And they said, "Surely he is covering his feet in the cool chamber."

25. And they waited until shame, and behold, he was not opening the doors of the upper-room. And they took the key and opened, and behold, their lord [was] fallen to the ground, dead.

26. And Ehud escaped while they delayed. And he passed the carved-stones and escaped to Seirah.

27. And it was when he came, and he blew the shofar in the hill-country of Ephrayim. And the sons of Yisrael went down with him from the hill-country, and he [was] before them.

28. And he said to them, "Pursue after me, for YHWH has given your enemies, Moav, into your hand." And they went down after him and seized the fords of the Yarden against Moav, and they did not allow a man to cross over.

29. And they struck Moav at that time — about ten thousand men, every stout [man] and every man of valor — and not a man escaped.

30. And Moav was subdued that day under the hand of Yisrael. And the land had rest eighty years.

31. And after him [was] Shamgar son of Anath, and he struck the Pelishtim — six hundred men — with an ox-goad. And he also saved Yisrael.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.8 — "Cushan-Rishatayim":** The name appears to mean "Cushan of Double Wickedness" — likely a pejorative distortion of the king's actual name.

2. **v.15 — "A man restricted in his right hand":** ish itter yad-y'mino — literally "a man bound/restricted of his right hand." This likely means left-handed, though some read it as having a disabled right hand. The detail is narratively crucial: a left-handed man would gird a sword on his right thigh, bypassing the guards' expected check of the left side.

3. **v.22 — "It came out the back":** The Hebrew parshedonah is a hapax legomenon — appearing only here in the entire Hebrew Bible. Its meaning is debated: "the back" (anal opening), "excrement came out," or "it came out at the front porch." The translation preserves the ambiguity.

4. **v.24 — "Covering his feet":** meissikh et-raglav — a euphemism for defecating (cf. 1 Sam 24:4). The servants assume Eglon is relieving himself in his private chamber.

— EMET, AI Translator
